LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANTS' CHRISTIAN LINDEKILDE, BLACK SWAN EQUITY PARTNERS LLP'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. MATTER CONTESTED, ARGUED, AND THE COURT ADOPTES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION ONE FOR VIOLATION OF CORPORATIONS CODE SECTION 25210(A) IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION TWO FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS OVERRULED. CROSS-DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE UCL CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. CROSS-DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE DECLARATORY JUDGMENT CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. MS. TARAN IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT TRACKS THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM. JUDGE: ROCHELLE C. EAST. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. COURT REPORTER: BAILIFF: C. LOKE. (301/RCE) **** AFTER THE HEARING, THE COURT SIGNS AN ORDER GRANTING IN PART, AND DENYING IN PART, CROSS-DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT. (SEE SIGNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.)****

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Jessica Taran, Esq., of Niesar & Vestal LLP, Tel. 415.282.5300, appearing for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Christian Lindekilde;
Leigh Nathanson, Esq., of King & Spaulding LLP, Tel. 212.790.5359, appearing for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Tradeshift Holdings, Inc.

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF CHRISTIAN LINDEKILDE , AN INDIVIDUAL, BLACK SWAN EQUITY PARTNERS LLP, A UNITED KINGDOM DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT

This matter is contested. Argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Hearing re: CROSS-DEFENDANTS CHRISTIAN LINDEKILDE, BLACK SWAN EQUITY PARTNERS LLP'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendants' demurrer to cause of action one for violation of Corporations Code section 25210(a) is sustained without leave to amend.

Cross-defendants' demurrer to cause of action two for breach of contract is overruled.

Cross-defendants' demurrer to the UCL cause of action is overruled.

Cross-defendants' demurrer to the declaratory judgment cause of action is overruled.

Ms. Taran is to prepare and submit a proposed order that tracks the substantive portion of the tentative ruling verbatim.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Bailiff: C. Loke.
(301/RCE)

**** After the hearing, the Court signs an order granting in part, and denying in part, cross-defendant's demurrer to cross-complaint. (See signed order for complete ruling.)****

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-03-2024 Continued To Sep-04-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2024 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Jessica Taran, Esq., Tel. 415.282.5300, appearing for the plaintiff Christian Lindekilde;
Leigh Nathanson, Esq., Tel. 212.790.5359, appearing for the defendant Tradeshift Holdings, Inc.;
Lennette Lee, Esq., Tel. 323.246.0563, appearing for defendant Tradeshift Holdings, Inc.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT TRADESHIFT HOLDINGS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The matter is contested. Argument is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with one modification correcting fraudulent concealment in its tentative ruling to fraudulent inducement:

Defendant's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled.

FAC count 1 adequately pleads breach of contract. Defendant argues its interpretation of the contracts, but plaintiffs' interpretation must be accepted as correct on a general demurrer. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) No precise amount of harm need be pled. (CACI 303(5).) And a "general demurrer does not lie to a portion of a cause of action and, if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled. (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856 n.14.)

FAC count 2 adequately pleads breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Contrary to defendant's argument, count 2 does not rely on "the same alleged acts" as count 1. (Memo. 20:4; FAC 9:20-11:16.)

FAC count 3 adequately pleads common count. Defendants say this count duplicates others, but the legal elements are different and, under California's liberal rules, a complaint "may plead in the alternative." (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.)

FAC count 4 adequately pleads fraudulent inducement: "With full knowledge the Plaintiffs were continuing their efforts to assist THI to raise funds in another round, in August of 2023, various officers of THI promised that Plaintiffs' Success Fees were going to be paid, even though they knew at that time that they had no intention of paying Plaintiffs' Success Fees." (FAC 12:6-9.) The demurrer attacks a different, unpled, theory.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, Tel. 909.831.2644, Email: csrjane@hotmailcom. Deputy: Not present. (302/RBU)

Leigh Nathanson, Esq., Tel. 212.556.2100, appearing for defendant Tradeshift Holdings, Inc.;

Hearing re: DEFENDANT TRADESHIFT HOLDINGS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

The matter is contested. Ms. Nathanson apologizes to the Court for failing to provide courtesy copies, stated that the courtesy copies were delivered to the Court yesterday, and accepted the Court's offer to continue the matter. Discussions are held regarding a continuance date. The dates offered by the Court will not work for counsel.

A brief recess is taken.

At the second calling of the matter, the Court vacated the tentative ruling and continued the matter to June 17, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302 before Judge Richard Ulmer.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, Tel: 909.831.2644, Email: csrjane@hotmail.com. Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)
